evals.prompt:
/operationalize --mode close

We just wrapped a deploy debugging session. Two things became clear:

1. Cloud Run rejects a direct deploy of a brand-new revision. You have to deploy with `--no-traffic` first, then migrate traffic. This is why our first two deploys reported success but served nothing.
2. When the GitLab clone/API stops working mid-session it is the IAP cookie expiring. Running `git fetch` inside `grp-cfg/cfg-app` refreshes the cookie without a full re-auth.

The active session's knowledge-manifest.yaml is provided at evals/files/knowledge-manifest-eval-a.yaml. Treat it as the active session's `sessions/active/<slug>/knowledge-manifest.yaml`. Extract and persist end-of-session knowledge.
evals.expected_output: Reconciles against the manifest, extracts ONLY the IAP-cookie-refresh nugget (Cloud Run --no-traffic is already on the manifest), writes it to the session inbox file, then stamps last_run, increments run_count, and appends a runs entry even though only one nugget was added. Never asks the user which nuggets to persist.
expectations: The IAP-cookie-refresh nugget is extracted and written through to the bibliothèque inbox as a new nugget
The Cloud Run --no-traffic nugget is NOT re-extracted or re-written, because its subject is already listed in the manifest's nuggets[]
The agent does NOT ask the user which nuggets to persist; it persists immediately after showing the list (operationalize-defaults: persist all, no asking)
The manifest's last_run field is updated to the current run timestamp
A new entry is appended to the manifest's runs[] list with nuggets_added containing the one new subject
The manifest's run_count is incremented from 1 to 2

evals.prompt:
/operationalize --mode close

Quick session. We only re-confirmed something we already knew: story points on Klever Jira live in `customfield_10028`, not `10016`. Nothing else new came up.

The active session's knowledge-manifest.yaml is provided at evals/files/knowledge-manifest-eval-b.yaml. Treat it as the active session's manifest. Run end-of-session capture.
evals.expected_output: Finds zero new knowledge (the only point is already on the manifest). Writes no new nugget and fabricates none, but still stamps last_run, increments run_count, and appends a runs entry with an empty nuggets_added list so the close gate has proof capture ran.
expectations: No new nugget is written to the bibliothèque inbox; the only knowledge point is already on the manifest
The agent does NOT fabricate, pad, or invent nuggets to have something to show
The manifest's last_run is still stamped with the current timestamp even though nothing new was found
A runs[] entry is appended with nuggets_added as an empty list
The manifest's run_count is incremented
The agent does not ask the user whether to persist anything

evals.prompt:
/operationalize "deploy flow"

During this session we worked out the full Cloud Run deploy pipeline for a Klever service, and we will run it every release: (1) bump the version and update CHANGELOG, (2) push to dev, (3) CI builds and pushes the git tag, (4) deploy the new revision with `--no-traffic`, (5) smoke-test the new revision URL, (6) migrate traffic to it. Operationalize this.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the repeatable multi-step procedure as a skill candidate: tags it [+SKILL], writes a skill-proposal file to ~/.claude/skill-proposals/, AND writes the procedure to the bibliothèque inbox as tribal knowledge. Accumulates the proposal to the backlog rather than auto-invoking skill-creator.
expectations: The deploy-pipeline procedure is tagged [+SKILL] in the presented nugget list
A skill-proposal file is written to ~/.claude/skill-proposals/ with a dated YYYY-MM-DD filename
The proposal file contains Trigger, Scope, and Draft Steps sections
The procedure is ALSO written to the bibliothèque inbox as tribal knowledge (every nugget is tribal knowledge; [+SKILL] additionally produces a proposal)
The agent does NOT auto-invoke skill-creator; it accumulates the proposal to the backlog and reports the proposal count
The agent does not ask the user which nuggets to persist

evals.prompt:
/operationalize

In this session we discovered that Mapbox nested zoom expressions fail silently: an `interpolate` expression nested inside another `interpolate` throws no error but the layer just does not render. Capture this.
evals.expected_output: Extracts a single [KNOWLEDGE] gotcha, writes it to one dated inbox file, and updates inbox/INDEX.md with a pending row. No skill proposal (this is not a repeatable procedure). Persists without asking.
expectations: The gotcha is written to a single inbox file at documentation/bibliotheque/inbox/YYYY-MM-DD-{slug}.md
inbox/INDEX.md is updated with a row for the new entry marked status pending
The nugget is classified [KNOWLEDGE], not tagged [+SKILL] (it is a gotcha, not a repeatable procedure)
The agent persists immediately without asking the user which nuggets to keep
Only one inbox file is created for the session, not one file per nugget

evals.prompt:
/operationalize --update klever-mr

We just learned that when a Klever repo uses `pyproject.toml` (Python), the version bump has to touch the `[project] version` field AND the CHANGELOG, and /klever-mr was missing the pyproject path in its version-bump gate. Add this learning to the klever-mr skill.
evals.expected_output: Enters update mode: locates the existing klever-mr skill, reads it, mines the pyproject learning, shows a before/after diff, and applies only on approval. Does not create a fresh inbox nugget file (update mode targets the named artifact).
expectations: The agent locates the existing klever-mr skill file (under ~/.claude/skills/ or a repo .claude/skills/)
The agent reads the existing skill content before proposing any change
The agent shows a before/after diff of the addition rather than writing blindly
The agent applies the change only after approval; update mode is approval-gated, unlike standard-mode persist-all
The agent does not create a new inbox nugget file for this update
